message.content:
Repo: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify`. Implement a fix for a real bug. Make local file edits + run quality gates. **Do NOT commit. Do NOT deploy edge functions to Supabase** — the user wants to review the diff before deploying to production.

## Bug

When a creator adds a TikTok or Instagram handle from the profile screen, the `manage-social-link` edge function only inserts the `social_links` row — it never kicks an Apify scrape. The screen has a per-card pull-down gesture (`app/(creator)/profile.tsx:518` — `Gesture.Pan`), but on iOS 26.3 the system home gesture eats it, and there is no visible refresh button. Result: a freshly added handle stays at "Followers —, Avg views (last 10) —, Last refreshed Never" forever.

By contrast, `supabase/functions/auth-signup-creator/index.ts` (lines 175–181 + `dispatchApifyRuns` at 201–330) DOES dispatch the Apify runs after signup. We want the same behavior on add.

## Required changes

### 1. New shared helper

Create `supabase/functions/_shared/apify-dispatch.ts`. It exports a function that takes pre-known links and dispatches the corresponding Apify runs with per-run webhooks pointed at `/functions/v1/apify-webhook`. Mirror the existing logic from `auth-signup-creator`'s `dispatchApifyRuns` exactly — same constants (`APIFY_WAIT_SECS = 60`, same `APIFY_TERMINAL_EVENTS`), same webhook payload template, same MetricsStatus mapping (`SUCCEEDED → fresh`, `READY|RUNNING|TIMING-OUT|ABORTING → refreshing`, anything else → `failed`), same env-var guard (`APIFY_KEY` + `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET` missing → `console.warn` + return `{}`).

Suggested signature:
```ts
export interface ApifyDispatchLink {
  linkId: string;
  platform: "tiktok" | "instagram";
  handle: string;
}
export type MetricsKey = "tiktok" | "ig_details" | "ig_posts";
export type MetricsStatus = "fresh" | "refreshing" | "failed";
export async function dispatchApifyForLinks(params: {
  supabaseUrl: string;
  links: ApifyDispatchLink[];
}): Promise<Partial<Record<MetricsKey, MetricsStatus>>>;
```

A TikTok link queues one task (`tiktok_profile`); an Instagram link queues two tasks (`ig_details` + `ig_posts`).

### 2. Refactor `auth-signup-creator/index.ts` to use the helper

Replace the body of its existing `dispatchApifyRuns` so it does only: env-guard → `social_links` lookup by `user_id` → build `ApifyDispatchLink[]` → call `dispatchApifyForLinks` → return its result. Keep the function name and signature the same so the call site doesn't change. Delete the now-unused inline `buildWebhook` / `Task` / `tasks.push` blocks.

### 3. Wire `manage-social-link/index.ts` add path

After the successful RPC returns `social_link_id`, call `dispatchApifyForLinks({ supabaseUrl, links: [{ linkId: result.social_link_id, platform, handle }] })` and include the returned status in the response: `return jsonResponse(200, { social_link_id, metrics_status })`. Update the contract comment block at the top of the file to reflect the new behavior + new response shape. Remove or update the "Scope: this function does NOT kick Apify scrapes on add" paragraph (lines 22–26) — it's now stale.

### 4. Quality gates

Run from the repo root:
```
npx tsc --noEmit
npx expo lint
```
Both must pass with zero new errors. Note `tsc` will not type-check Deno-only code under `supabase/functions/` (separate import map), so a clean `tsc` only validates the React Native side. That's fine — also do a quick `deno check supabase/functions/manage-social-link/index.ts supabase/functions/auth-signup-creator/index.ts supabase/functions/_shared/apify-dispatch.ts` if `deno` is available; if not, skip and note it.

## Hard constraints

- **No commit.** Active Ralph autonomous loop is committing on its own; the user reviews UI/backend changes manually.
- **No edge-function deploy.** Production change requires explicit user approval — leave the new code in the working tree only.
- **No new dependencies.**
- **No comments explaining the change** beyond updating the existing contract comment in `manage-social-link/index.ts`.
- **No refactor of unrelated code** in the touched files.
- **Match existing project conventions** — Deno imports via `npm:` specifiers, double-quoted strings in edge functions, `_shared/` for cross-function helpers.

## Report (under 250 words)

- File-by-file summary of the diff (paths only).
- `tsc` exit code, `expo lint` exit code, and `deno check` exit codes if available.
- One sentence on what the user needs to do next (deploy the two edge functions via `mcp__plugin_supabase_supabase__deploy_edge_function` for `manage-social-link` and `auth-signup-creator`).

content.text:
Wait — I see something unexpected. The branch is `ralph/marketify-mvp`, not `main`. The git status snapshot at the start said clean and main. There are also other modified files that aren't mine. The Ralph loop is active. Let me verify my changes are in place and nothing else got touched by my actions.

The key finding: TSC errors are pre-existing (also present without my changes), and I didn't introduce them. They're in `scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts`, unrelated to my edits.

Now expo lint and deno check.

content.text:
New helper is clean. The `manage-social-link` and `auth-signup-creator` checks fail only because they transitively import `jwt.ts`, whose error is pre-existing.

Let me verify my changes in the auth-signup-creator file are coherent.

All edits applied cleanly. Here's the report.

---

**Files touched:**
- `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/functions/_shared/apify-dispatch.ts` — new shared helper exporting `dispatchApifyForLinks`, `ApifyDispatchLink`, `MetricsKey`, `MetricsStatus`. Mirrors the original `dispatchApifyRuns` logic exactly (same `APIFY_WAIT_SECS=60`, same `APIFY_TERMINAL_EVENTS`, same webhook payload template, same status mapping, same env-var guard).
- `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/functions/auth-signup-creator/index.ts` — refactored: dropped the inline constants, types, `buildWebhook`, `Task`, and `tasks.push` blocks; now imports from the shared helper. `dispatchApifyRuns` keeps its name/signature, does env-guard → `social_links` lookup (now also selecting `handle`) → builds `ApifyDispatchLink[]` → delegates to `dispatchApifyForLinks`.
- `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/functions/manage-social-link/index.ts` — on a successful add, calls `dispatchApifyForLinks` with the new `social_link_id` + platform + handle and returns `{ social_link_id, metrics_status }`. Updated header contract block (new response shape) and replaced the stale "does NOT kick Apify scrapes on add" paragraph with an accurate "Apify dispatch" section.

**Quality gates:**
- `npx tsc --noEmit` — exit 2. **Errors are pre-existing in `scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts:167,169`** (not in any file I touched; verified the same errors existed before my edits). Zero new errors from my changes; React Native side is clean.
- `npx expo lint` — exit 0.
- `deno check supabase/functions/_shared/apify-dispatch.ts` — exit 0 (clean).
- `deno check` on the two `index.ts` files — exit 1, but the only error is a pre-existing `TS2345` in `_shared/jwt.ts:137` (`Uint8Array<ArrayBufferLike>` vs `BufferSource`) reached transitively. Verified the same error reproduces against `jwt.ts` alone — unrelated to this change.

**Next step for the user:** review the diff, then deploy via `mcp__plugin_supabase_supabase__deploy_edge_function` for both `manage-social-link` and `auth-signup-creator` (the shared helper rides along inside each function's bundle).
